results.question: List all owners in Moscow, Idaho who have the lowest annual income among Moscow owners, and among those lowest-income owners return only the owner or owners with the largest number of pets.
results.query_family_reason: has/contains ALL members of a set (count-distinct division)
results.sql:
WITH MoscowOwners AS (
    SELECT o.owner_id, o.owner_name, o.annual_income
    FROM owners o
    WHERE o.city = 'Moscow' AND o.state = 'Idaho'
),
MinIncome AS (
    SELECT MIN(annual_income) AS min_income
    FROM MoscowOwners
),
LowestIncomeOwners AS (
    SELECT mo.owner_id, mo.owner_name, mo.annual_income
    FROM MoscowOwners mo
    JOIN MinIncome mi ON mo.annual_income = mi.min_income
),
PetCounts AS (
    SELECT p.owner_id, COUNT(*) AS pet_count
    FROM pets p
    WHERE p.owner_id IN (SELECT owner_id FROM LowestIncomeOwners)
    GROUP BY p.owner_id
),
MaxPetCount AS (
    SELECT MAX(pet_count) AS max_pet_count
    FROM PetCounts
)
SELECT lo.owner_id, lo.owner_name, lo.annual_income
FROM LowestIncomeOwners lo
JOIN PetCounts pc ON lo.owner_id = pc.owner_id
JOIN MaxPetCount mpc ON pc.pet_count = mpc.max_pet_count

FROM foods f

        FROM purchases p3

        WHERE f3.food_type = f.food_type
    )
)
GROUP BY f.food_type
